MARINE ENGINEERING ROADMAP

MARINE ENGINEERING students need practical, industry-relevant skills that connect classroom knowledge to internships, projects, professional practice, and global career opportunities.

MARINE ENGINES

MARINE ENGINES is a practical skill that helps MARINE ENGINEERING students prepare for real professional work.

SHIP SYSTEMS

SHIP SYSTEMS is a practical skill that helps MARINE ENGINEERING students prepare for real professional work.

MAINTENANCE ENGINEERING

MAINTENANCE ENGINEERING is a practical skill that helps MARINE ENGINEERING students prepare for real professional work.

MARINE ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS

MARINE ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS is a practical skill that helps MARINE ENGINEERING students prepare for real professional work.

HYDRAULICS

HYDRAULICS is a practical skill that helps MARINE ENGINEERING students prepare for real professional work.

SAFETY MANAGEMENT

SAFETY MANAGEMENT is a practical skill that helps MARINE ENGINEERING students prepare for real professional work.

TECHNICAL DRAWING

TECHNICAL DRAWING is a practical skill that helps MARINE ENGINEERING students prepare for real professional work.

TROUBLESHOOTING

TROUBLESHOOTING is a practical skill that helps MARINE ENGINEERING students prepare for real professional work.

QUALITY CONTROL

QUALITY CONTROL is a practical skill that helps MARINE ENGINEERING students prepare for real professional work.

ENERGY EFFICIENCY

ENERGY EFFICIENCY is a practical skill that helps MARINE ENGINEERING students prepare for real professional work.

WHY THESE SKILLS MATTER

PROFESSIONAL READINESS

These skills help students move from theory to practical work.

EMPLOYER DEMAND

Employers value graduates who can use tools, solve problems, and communicate results.

CAREER GROWTH

Building these skills early creates stronger opportunities for internships, projects, and jobs.
